english: well of religion. 2. = gśin-rje (gshin rje) Schl. Buddh. 93, 3. also as a p. n. — čos-rgyúd (chos rgyud) religious tradition, also = confession, creed, rje-btsún-gyi čos-rgyúd ºdzin-pa-rnams (rje btsun gyi chos rgyud 'dzin pa rnams) those embracing the religious tradition of his reverence, his fellow-believers Mil.; čos-rgyúd gčig-pa (chos rgyud gcig pa) one confessing the same faith or religion Thgr. — čós-čan (chos can) 1. pious, devout. 2. v. čos (chos) 5, ºjig-pai čós-čan yin ('jig pa'i chos can yin) having the properties of perishableness, being subject to the law of mutability Thgy. — čos-rjé (chos rje) 'lord of the faith', viz. 1. Buddha Lex., 2. devout or righteous lord, title of honour given to distinguished scholars Tar. transl. 331, and elsewh.; perh. also = čos-rgyál (chos rgyal). — čos-nyíd (chos nyid) 1. = čos (chos) 5, quality, nature, rgyá-mtsoi čos-nyíd-kyis (rgya mtsho'i chos nyid kyis) in a manner peculiar to the sea, Dzl. 72, 9 (112, 9?). 2. philosophical term: existence, entity, = de-bźin-nyíd (de bzhin nyid) (acc. to Thgy.) by which the Buddhist however means a negation of being, non-existence, non-entity. — čos-stégs (chos stegs) W. = čos-kri (chos khri). — čos-stón (chos ston) religious festive entertainment given to saints Glr. — čos-drán-po (chos drang po) righteous with regard to the laws of religion (adopted by Prot. Miss. for the scriptural term 'righteous' or 'just'), čos-drán-ba (chos drang ba) justice, righteousness. — čos-ldán (chos ldan) = čós-čan (chos can). — čos-sdé (chos sde) convent, monastery, Wdk., Glr. — čós-pa (chos pa) a religious man, a divine, a monk. — čos-spún (chos spun) a religious brother; such brotherhoods e.g. are formed by two devotees, before going on a pilgrimage. After having been consecrated by a priest, who consults the lot on such an occasion, they owe hospitality and mutual assistance to each other for life. — čos-spyód (chos spyod) exercise of religion; čos-spyód-bču (chos spyod bcu) = dgé-ba-bču (dge ba bcu). — čos-sbyín (chos sbyin) is said to be frq used in book-titles: bkra-śis-lhùn-po-nas čos-sbyín ºdzad-méd spel p̌yir bris (bkra shis lhun po nas chos sbyin 'dzad med spel phyir bris) written from Tashilhunpo as a religious gift for infinite increase and blessing. — čos-blón (chos blon) a pious functionary or official (bdud-blon (bdud blon) an impious or wicked one) Glr. čós-ma (chos ma) a religious woman, a nun Cs. — čos-méd (chos med) without religion, irreligious, wicked. — čos-myón (chos myon) religious frenzy, W.: *čos-nyón źugs* (*chos nyon zhugs*) he has become deranged, his brains are turned (in consequence of meditating). — čos-zóg (chos zog) priestcraft Mil. = čos-lúgs (chos lugs) religious party, denomination, sect. *čós-sem-čan* (*chos sem can*) W. inclined to religion, pious.

Lex.: = 'tomb, sepulchre; = pramārita Ssk. killed, slain; mčád-pa-med-pa (mchad pa med pa) entire, perfect; mčád-par byú-ba (mchad par byu ba) = mahimān Ssk. greatness; also the magical power of increasing size at will'.

1. the side of the breast, mčán-gyi bu (mchan gyi bu) bosom-child, darling, mčán-gyi mčis-brán (mchan gyi mchis bran) bosom-wife (cf. our 'bosom-friend'); mčán-du ºjúg-pa (mchan du 'jug pa) to put into one's bosom Glr.; mčán-kuṅ (mchan khung) arm-hole, arm-pit, often = mčan (mchan); mčán-kuṅ gyás-pai rtsib-mai bár nas (mchan khung g-yas pa'i rtsib ma'i bar nas) (the Buddhas are born) from between the ribs of the right side (cf. mṅal (mngal)); *čán-da* (*chan da*) W. pocket, in clothes, cf. dkú-mda (dku mda). — 2. v. the following article.

1. apprentice, bzoi (bzo'i) in a handicraft, trade or art, rig-pai (rig pa'i) in a science, disciple Cs., sgyú-ma-mkán-gyi (sgyu ma mkhan gyi) appr. of a juggler, conjurer Zam. — 2. yi-géi mčán-bu (yi ge'i mchan bu) words or lines, printed or written in a smaller character than the rest, and inserted in the text (called má-yig (ma yig) Cs.) like our parenthesis, but without brackets; hence 3. note, annotation (Sch. also: testimony?).

eleg. for 1. to come, to go, slád-bźin-par mčío (slad bzhin par mchi'o) I shall come later Dzl.; to appear, used of a god; skyábs-su (skyabs su) (to put one's self) under the protection of another person, ccd.; ºbáṅs-su mčío ('bangs su mchi'o) I will obey Mil. — 2. to say, źes mčío (zhes mchi'o) thus he said.

resp. spyan-čáb (spyan chab) a tear, ºbyín-pa ('byin pa), ºdón-pa ('don pa) Glr., blág-pa (blag pa) Dzl., gtóṅ-ba (gtong ba) Mil. to shed (tears); ském-pa (skem pa) to dry up tears Cs.; ºp̌yí-ba ('phyi ba) to wipe off tears Cs.; mčí-mas brnáṅ-ba (mchi mas brnang ba) to be choked with tears, to sob violently Sch.

1. Cs. a stone for grinding spice etc., a mortar; mčíg-gu (mchig gu) a small mortar Sch., a pestle Cs. — 2. the nether mill-stone, mčíg-ma (mchig ma) the runner or upper mill-stone, Sch., mčíg skór-ba (mchig skor ba) to grind Sch.
